user.title: Automation & Script Manager
user.description: Do you want to set up the Automation & Script Manager custom integration?

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only a single instance of this integration is allowed.

init.title: Configure Options
init.description: Configure the global settings for Automation & Script Manager.

data.prompt_one_time_vs_recurring: Prompt LLM to be explicit about one-time vs recurring
data.restrict_deletion: Restrict deletion to tagged entities only

data_description.tag: The tag (label name) to assign automatically to all created automations/scripts. If the label doesn't exist, it will be registered in Home Assistant. (Only alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.)
data_description.one_shot_tag: A second tag (label name) to assign specifically to temporary/one-shot entities (i.e. those with a self-destruct completion action), in addition to the global entity tag. (Only alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.)
data_description.expose_llm_tools: Expose the creation and deletion actions as tools to conversation LLM agents.
data_description.let_llm_assign_icon: If enabled, the AI assistant will be allowed to choose custom icons for created/updated entities and will be equipped with the GetCommonIcons search tool.
data_description.prompt_one_time_vs_recurring: If enabled, the LLM will be explicitly instructed to state in its speech response whether the generated automation is one-time or recurring.
data_description.debug_mode: If enabled, LLM intent handlers will accept an optional reasoning parameter, log it to the system log, and include it in the response.
data_description.categorize_mode: Choose how to handle categorization of generated entities: 'Leave uncategorized', 'Put in specified category' (requires next step to pick categories), or 'Auto-categorize' (requires next step for fallback logic). If no categories exist in Home Assistant, only 'Leave uncategorized' will be available.
data_description.restrict_deletion: If enabled, delete actions will fail for any automation or script that does not have the specified tag.
data_description.disable_instead_of_delete: If enabled, automations and scripts that would be deleted (either via service call or self-destruct completion action) are disabled/modified to prevent execution rather than removed.
data_description.would_be_deleted_tag: The tag (label name) to assign specifically to entities that have been disabled instead of deleted. (Only alphanumeric characters, spaces, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.)

category_specified.title: Select Specific Categories
category_specified.description: Select the default categories that will be assigned to all newly created or updated automations and scripts when 'Put in specified category' categorization mode is active.

data_description.specified_automation_category: The specific category to assign automatically to all generated automations. Choose 'None' to leave automations uncategorized.
data_description.specified_script_category: The specific category to assign automatically to all generated scripts. Choose 'None' to leave scripts uncategorized.

category_auto.title: Auto-Categorization Settings
category_auto.description: Configure settings for the dynamic AI-driven categorization behavior.

data_description.always_assign_category: If enabled, the LLM is strictly required to pick the closest category ID from the list of existing categories. If disabled, the LLM is allowed to leave the entity uncategorized if it determines that no category is a strong fit.

security.title: Allowed Actions Security Policy
security.description: Restrict what service actions can be executed by AI conversation agents. Lines starting with # are ignored.

data_description.disallow_regexes: List of regular expressions (one per line, e.g. 'homeassistant\..*') specifying blocked actions. Checked first; matches are immediately blocked. Lines starting with # are treated as comments.
data_description.allow_regexes: List of regular expressions (one per line, e.g. 'light\..*') specifying allowed actions. If specified, only actions matching one or more rules will be permitted. Lines starting with # are treated as comments.
